subject: Gaming
title: Gaming and Mental Health During COVID
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Gaming and Mental Health During COVID.

hook.text: During lockdowns, gaming became a lifeline. Animal Crossing replaced real-world social interaction.

buildup.text: With physical socializing banned, multiplayer games became the primary way millions stayed connected.

discovery.text: Studies found that gaming during COVID reduced loneliness and improved mood for isolated individuals.

twist.text: The WHO—which had classified gaming disorder—now encouraged gaming as a way to stay home safely.

climax.text: COVID proved that gaming's social benefits are real and measurable during times of crisis.

punchline.text: The WHO said gaming was a disorder. Then they said it was a lifeline.

quiz.question: How did the WHO's stance on gaming change during COVID?
options: They encouraged gaming as a way to stay connected during lockdowns
They banned all gaming during lockdowns
